description_long: This is a weathered, industrial-style traffic light with a rectangular yellow metal housing and three large cylindrical signal compartments stacked vertically. The surface is covered in black grime and scuff marks, suggesting long-term outdoor exposure. Each compartment is recessed, with a hooded visor to shield the lights from sunlight and rain. The side views reveal the cylindrical depth of each signal section and the bolted construction along the edges. The back is flat and featureless, likely designed for mounting against a pole or wall. The overall appearance is robust and utilitarian, typical of traffic control devices found at urban intersections.
description: A weathered yellow metal traffic light with three vertically stacked cylindrical signal compartments and visible grime, designed for urban intersections.
description_view_0: Front view showing three open, hooded signal compartments stacked vertically, with visible grime and wear.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the cylindrical depth of the three signal lights and the bolted edge construction.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a flat, featureless yellow metal back with visible weathering and no signal lights.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the cylindrical signal compartments and the mounting structure.
